# `UDP C->S 0x03/0x27` — Reliable/RequestWorldInfo

**Transport:** UDP  
**Direction:** C->S  
**Identifier:** `0x03/0x27`  
**Status:** verified

## Structure

Client request for entity info — "tell me about world object N".
Fixed 5-byte body (post-`[03][seq LE2]` wrapper). Verified
2026-05-10 against 126 samples from 5 retail captures.

```
[0]      0x27                   sub-opcode (constant)
[1..4]   world_obj_id LE32      target NPC/object mapID (typically
                                 0x101..0x1FF — the retail NPC range)
```

Sample retail bytes (catalog):
```
27 24 01 00 00    request info about NPC 0x0124 (= 292)
27 4a 01 00 00    request info about NPC 0x014a (= 330)
27 fc 03 00 00    request info about NPC 0x03fc (= 1020)
```

The high byte is `0x00` in all 126 samples — entity IDs stay
within 0x0000-0xFFFF (16-bit logical range) even though the wire
field is LE32.

## Variants

Single 5-byte form across all 2,942 retail observations. NO size
variation.

## Observed contexts

Client emits when the player's view-frustum contains an NPC the
client doesn't yet have full info for (typical mouseover / new
zone-resident NPC walk-into-view). Server responds with a S→C
{@code 0x03/0x28} {@link
server.gameserver.packets.server_udp.WorldNPCInfo} carrying the
NPC's name/type/position/textures.

Per-capture entity_id stability: AUGUSTO has 0x0124 / 0x014a /
0x0121 / 0x03fc / 0x03f0 (mix of NPC IDs 292, 330, 289, 1020,
1008); CREATION has 0x0102 / 0x0101 / 0x0100 / 0x0102 / 0x0102
(repeated requests for nearby NPCs). The client polls
intermittently as it scans the local zone.

## Open questions

None remaining — packet is fully decoded. The server-side response
({@code 0x03/0x28} WorldNPCInfo) is documented separately.

## Server-side handler

Decoded via {@link
server.gameserver.packets.client_udp.RequestInfoAboutWordlID}:
- {@code skip(1)} past 0x27
- {@code worldObjId = readInt()} LE32
- {@code execute()} looks up the NPC by mapID and emits a
  {@code WorldNPCInfo} response. If the NPC isn't found, drops
  silently (logged as `RequestWorldInfo for unknown objectId=...`
  at Info level).

Tests:
- `RequestInfoAboutWorldIDTest` — pins the LE32 decode and
  WorldNPCInfo emit on known/unknown NPCs.
